title: Bignum arithmetic — promote i64 overflow to heap-allocated BigInt
purpose: beamr arithmetic BIFs use i64::checked_* and raise badarith on overflow. BEAM supports arbitrary-precision integers. Gleam programs that compute factorial(20), large timestamps, or cryptographic values crash. The BigInt heap type already exists (BoxedTag::BigInt, write_bigint, BigInt accessor) from ETF/constant-pool support, but arithmetic never promotes to it. This brief wires overflow into BigInt allocation and extends comparison, hashing, and ETF encode/decode to handle mixed small-int/bigint operands.
task: Extend the arithmetic BIFs (+, -, *, div, rem, band, bor, bxor, bsl, bsr, bnot) to detect i64 overflow and promote the result to a heap-allocated BigInt. Extend comparison (erlang:'<'/2, erlang:'=:='/2, etc.) to handle BigInt operands. Extend term hashing and ETF encode/decode to support BigInt round-trips. Use a multi-limb unsigned magnitude + sign representation (already the BigInt heap layout). No external crate dependency — implement multi-limb add/sub/mul/divmod directly.

requirements.title: Arithmetic overflow promotes to BigInt
requirements.spec: When i64::checked_add/sub/mul/div returns None (overflow), compute the correct result using multi-limb arithmetic and allocate a BigInt on the process heap via alloc_bigint. The BIF returns the BigInt term instead of raising badarith. Small results that fit in i64 after bignum computation are demoted back to small_int.
acceptance: factorial(20) returns 2432902008176640000 as a small_int (fits i64)
factorial(21) returns the correct value as a BigInt term
factorial(100) returns the correct 158-digit value
addition of i64::MAX + 1 returns a BigInt
subtraction of i64::MIN - 1 returns a BigInt
multiplication overflow promotes correctly
small result after bignum operation demotes to small_int

requirements.title: Multi-limb arithmetic primitives
requirements.spec: Implement add, subtract, multiply, and divmod for multi-limb unsigned integers with sign handling. Each limb is u64. Use schoolbook algorithms (no need for Karatsuba at this stage). Place in a new crates/beamr/src/term/bigint_math.rs module.
acceptance: multi-limb add with carry propagation produces correct results
multi-limb subtract with borrow produces correct results
multi-limb multiply (schoolbook) produces correct results for up to 64 limbs
divmod produces correct quotient and remainder
sign handling: negative * negative = positive, etc.
zero normalization: leading zero limbs are stripped

requirements.title: Comparison and ordering for BigInt
requirements.spec: Extend term comparison (compare::partial_cmp, compare::partial_eq) to handle BigInt vs BigInt and BigInt vs small_int comparisons. BigInt ordering follows numeric value, matching BEAM semantics where integers of any size are ordered by value.
acceptance: BigInt == BigInt with same value returns true
BigInt < BigInt with smaller value returns true
BigInt > small_int when BigInt value exceeds i64 range
small_int == BigInt when values are equal (after demotion check)
ordering is consistent with Erlang term ordering

requirements.title: Term hashing for BigInt
requirements.spec: Extend term hashing to include BigInt values. BigInt hashing must be consistent with small_int hashing for values that fit in i64 (so a BigInt demoted to small_int hashes the same). Use the limb representation for values outside i64 range.
acceptance: hash(BigInt(42)) == hash(small_int(42))
hash(BigInt(i64::MAX + 1)) is deterministic and non-zero
distinct BigInt values produce distinct hashes (spot check)

requirements.title: ETF encode/decode for BigInt
requirements.spec: Extend ETF encoder to emit SMALL_BIG_EXT or LARGE_BIG_EXT for BigInt terms. Extend ETF decoder to read these tags and allocate BigInt on the target heap. Round-trip: encode(BigInt) |> decode == original BigInt.
acceptance: BigInt encodes to SMALL_BIG_EXT for values with <= 255 bytes
BigInt encodes to LARGE_BIG_EXT for larger values
decode(encode(factorial(100))) == factorial(100)
SMALL_BIG_EXT from external BEAM source decodes correctly
negative BigInt encode/decode round-trips

requirements.title: Bitwise operations for BigInt
requirements.spec: Extend band, bor, bxor, bsl, bsr, bnot BIFs to handle BigInt operands. Mixed small_int/BigInt operands promote the small_int to BigInt for the operation.
acceptance: band of two BigInt values produces correct result
bsl of small_int by large shift produces BigInt
bsr of BigInt back to small range demotes to small_int
bnot of BigInt produces correct two's complement result

boundaries: Implement multi-limb arithmetic directly — no external bignum crate
Schoolbook algorithms are sufficient — Karatsuba/FFT optimization deferred
BigInt GC integration uses existing boxed-term tracing — no new GC infrastructure
JIT compilation of BigInt arithmetic deferred — interpreter path only
OUT OF SCOPE: Rational numbers or arbitrary-precision floats
OUT OF SCOPE: Performance parity with GMP — correctness first
OUT OF SCOPE: Crypto-specific constant-time arithmetic
verification: ETF round-trip for large integers matches BEAM output
no badarith for overflow in arithmetic BIFs
